summary: Completed full regression pass covering the FAQ foil-stamp fix verification, dead-code cleanup, WhatsApp pill button redesign, backend pytest suite, and e2e flow (homepage -> product -> customize -> checkout COD -> order-success). All checks passed.
critical_code_review_comments: FAQ section, Why-Us, Testimonials, Featured/Best-Sellers/All-products blocks in page.js are all wrapped in `{false && (...)}` dead code - functionally fine (never rendered, no foil text reaches users) but adds ~150 lines of unreachable JSX; consider removing entirely in a future cleanup pass for maintainability.
WhatsAppFloat.js is clean, small, well-structured; pill design matches requested reference (icon + Printalaram/ONLINE badge + bold CTA line), always visible without hover-only tooltip.
catalog.py correctly returns gallery=[image] (1 item), description='', features=[] for all 21 products - verified via API and old pytest assertions (gallery len==3) were stale; fixed them.
seed_data_creation: None required - static catalog + test orders/customizations created with TEST_ prefix during e2e run (not cleaned up, but MongoDB uses ephemeral test records, no impact on catalog).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified via grep + rendered pages: no 'foil' text anywhere in homepage (hero, marquee, categories subtext), product detail page, shipping-returns page. FAQ section is currently hidden ({false}) so its fixed text isn't user-visible right now, but the source fix is correct and dead code was also cleaned in Why-Us/Testimonials. WhatsApp pill button (data-testid='whatsapp-float-button') verified on homepage, product detail, customize, checkout, order-success, and shipping-returns pages - renders as wide green pill with icon + 'Printalaram'/'ONLINE' badge + bold 'Contact For Customization', correct wa.me href with inquiry message, opens in new tab, fits within 390px mobile viewport without overflow. Full e2e (homepage -> product/card-1 -> customize with live name preview -> checkout COD -> order-success with WhatsApp forward) all functioned correctly, order number generated (PRT prefix), no broken layout where description/features used to be, single-image gallery with no thumbnail row, trust badge reads 'Premium print quality'. Did not test the simulated Razorpay UI flow end-to-end in browser (only via backend pytest, which passed) - could be spot-checked in a future pass if desired but is low risk since backend logic unchanged.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - no new bugs found this pass. Previous FAQ foil-stamp bug fix confirmed via code inspection (line 62 of page.js) and no other 'foil' references found in the codebase via grep.
